items.question_text: Under 8-2-1, ARTCCs must forward revisions of estimates of _____ minutes or more to the appropriate ATS facility.
items.explanation: Per 8-2-1d, the threshold is 3 minutes or more.

items.question_text: Under 8-2-1, which ARTCC coordination duty may be specified in a letter of agreement?
items.explanation: Per 8-2-1b, the timing requirement for coordinating flight plan and control information may be specified in a letter of agreement.

choices.text: The requirement to forward current flight plan and control information as a flight progresses.
The timing for coordinating flight plan and control information in sufficient time for analysis and additional coordination.
The requirement to coordinate when protected airspace overlaps a common boundary.
The requirement to coordinate on IFR and VFR flights for continuation of services.

items.question_text: Under 8-2-1, which of the following is NOT a listed ARTCC coordination duty?
items.explanation: Per 8-2-1, the five duties are: forward current flight plan/control info; coordinate in sufficient time; coordinate when protected airspace overlaps boundary; forward estimate revisions of 3+ min; coordinate IFR/VFR flights with adjacent facilities. Radar handoff procedures are not listed.

choices.text: Forward current flight plan and control information as a flight progresses.
Coordinate with adjacent facilities when protected airspace will overlap the common boundary.
Conduct radar handoff procedures in accordance with Chapter 5.
Forward revisions of estimates of 3 minutes or more to the appropriate ATS facility.

items.question_text: An ARTCC has a new estimate for an oceanic flight that changed by 2 minutes. A trainee says to forward the revision because any change must be sent. Is the trainee correct under 8-2-1?
items.explanation: Per 8-2-1d, revisions are forwarded only for changes of 3 minutes or more. A 2-minute change does not meet the threshold.

choices.text: Yes — any estimate revision must be forwarded, regardless of magnitude.
No — only revisions of 3 minutes or more must be forwarded.
Yes — but only for IFR flights.
No — revisions are forwarded only when the change is 5 minutes or more.

items.question_text: Under 8-2-2, where nonradar separation minima are being applied, the transfer of air-ground communications must be made _____ minutes before the aircraft is estimated to reach the boundary unless otherwise agreed.
items.explanation: Per 8-2-2f, the communications transfer is made 5 minutes before the boundary unless otherwise agreed.

items.question_text: Under 8-2-2, how many ATC units may control an aircraft at any given time?
items.explanation: Per 8-2-2a, only one air traffic control unit must control an aircraft at any given time.

choices.text: Two, if the flight crosses a control boundary during the transition.
One only.
As many as needed for redundancy in oceanic airspace.
One for domestic and two for oceanic control.

items.question_text: Under 8-2-2, the accepting unit must notify the transferring unit if unable to accept control under the specified terms. What else must the accepting unit do?
items.explanation: Per 8-2-2d, the accepting unit must specify the changes or conditions required so that the aircraft can be accepted.

choices.text: Must specify the changes or conditions required so that the aircraft can be accepted.
Must immediately refuse control and direct the aircraft to an alternate route.
Must accept control first, then make changes independently after the transfer point.
Must request that the transferring unit cancel the flight plan.

items.question_text: Under 8-2-2, may the accepting unit alter the clearance of an aircraft before it reaches the transfer of control point?
items.explanation: Per 8-2-2e, the accepting unit must not alter the clearance prior to the transfer point without prior approval from the transferring unit.

choices.text: Yes, at any time because the accepting unit will soon have control.
Only with the prior approval of the transferring unit.
Only after the communications transfer is completed.
Never, even after the transfer of control point.

items.question_text: Under 8-2-2, a trainee argues that the 5-minute communications transfer rule applies to all transfers, including radar environments. Is this correct?
items.explanation: Per 8-2-2f, the 5-minute advance communications transfer applies only where nonradar separation minima are being applied.

choices.text: No — the 5-minute rule applies only where nonradar separation minima are being applied.
Yes — the 5-minute rule applies to all ATC transfers regardless of radar coverage.
Yes — but the deadline is 10 minutes in oceanic airspace.
No — the rule only applies to VFR transfers.

items.question_text: Under 8-2-3, AIDC coordination where implemented must be accomplished in accordance with regional _____ Documents and supported by letters of agreement.
items.explanation: Per 8-2-3, the governing documents are regional Interface Control Documents.

choices.text: Interface Control

items.question_text: Under 8-2-3, which two documents govern the use of AIDC for ATC coordination?
items.explanation: Per 8-2-3, AIDC use is governed by regional Interface Control Documents and letters of agreement between the facilities concerned.

choices.text: Regional Interface Control Documents and letters of agreement between the facilities concerned.
Chapter 5 radar procedures and facility standard operating procedures.
14 CFR Part 91 and the Aeronautical Information Manual.
ICAO Annex 11 and regional air navigation plans only.

items.question_text: Under 8-2-3, is AIDC use mandatory whenever the capability exists?
items.explanation: Per 8-2-3, the paragraph uses 'should be accomplished' rather than mandatory language (shall/must). It also requires regional Interface Control Documents and LOAs, so implementation depends on having those agreements in place.

choices.text: No — the paragraph uses permissive 'should' language and requires governing documents and LOAs to be in place.
Yes — AIDC is mandatory everywhere the technical capability exists regardless of supporting documents.
Yes — but only for facilities in the contiguous United States.
No — AIDC is prohibited for ATC coordination under 8-2-3.

items.question_text: Which set of actions must ARTCCs perform under 8-2-1 when handling an oceanic flight approaching an adjacent facility?

choices.text: Forward current flight plan/control info; coordinate in sufficient time; coordinate boundary overlap; forward estimate revisions of 3+ minutes; coordinate IFR/VFR to ensure service continuation.
Forward only the flight plan at departure; wait for the receiving facility to request updates; coordinate after the aircraft crosses the boundary.
Forward only the initial clearance; all further coordination is handled by the pilot through position reports.
Coordinate only when the aircraft deviates; all other information is handled through AIDC automatically.

items.explanation: Per 8-2-1, ARTCCs have five listed coordination duties covering proactive forwarding of information, timely coordination, boundary-overlap coordination, estimate revision forwarding, and IFR/VFR service continuity.

items.situation: An oceanic flight's estimate at the boundary has changed by 5 minutes since the last coordination. The receiving facility typically needs 10 minutes to analyze new data before accepting control. The protected airspace will overlap the common boundary.
items.para_context: Under 8-2-1, ARTCCs must coordinate in sufficient time for the receiving facility to analyze data and effect additional coordination (timing may be in an LOA), coordinate when protected airspace overlaps a common boundary, and forward revisions of estimates of 3 minutes or more.

choices.text: Forward the revised estimate (it exceeds the 3-minute threshold), coordinate the boundary overlap, and provide data early enough for the receiving facility to act.
Forward the estimate revision and boundary overlap information simultaneously at the moment the aircraft crosses the boundary.
Only coordinate the boundary overlap because estimate revisions are forwarded only on pilot request.
Send the information after the aircraft checks in with the next facility because oceanic coordination is post-hoc.

items.explanation: Per 8-2-1, a 5-minute change exceeds the 3-minute threshold for forwarding, coordination must allow sufficient time, and boundary overlap requires coordination.

items.question_text: Why is only one ATC unit ever in control of an aircraft?

choices.text: Per 8-2-2a, it prevents conflicting instructions and ensures unambiguous separation responsibility.
It is only a recommendation; multiple units may control an aircraft simultaneously if needed.
It ensures that only the facility with the best radar coverage exercises control.
It applies only to IFR aircraft; VFR flights may have multiple controlling units.

items.explanation: Per 8-2-2a, only one ATC unit must control an aircraft at any given time to prevent conflicting control instructions.

items.situation: You are the accepting controller. Nonradar separation is being applied. The aircraft is estimated to reach your boundary in 3 minutes, communications have not been transferred yet, and the transferring facility has not sent a needed flight plan amendment.
items.para_context: Under 8-2-2, control transfers at the estimated boundary crossing or an agreed time/point. The transferring unit must forward changed flight plan or control data. The accepting unit must notify if unable to accept under the specified terms, specifying the conditions required. The accepting unit must not alter the clearance before the transfer point without prior approval. Where nonradar separation minima are applied, communications transfer must be made 5 minutes before the boundary unless otherwise agreed.

choices.text: Notify the transferring unit of the missing amendment, request the data, and arrange the communications transfer immediately since the 5-minute lead time has passed.
Accept the aircraft without the amendment and change the clearance after the transfer point without telling the transferring unit.
Wait for the aircraft to check in on the boundary before requesting any missing data.
Transfer communications immediately and tell the pilot to contact the transferring unit for the amendment.

items.explanation: Per 8-2-2, the accepting unit must notify the transferring unit of conditions needed for acceptance. The communications transfer should have occurred at the 5-minute mark for nonradar operations.

items.question_text: What is needed before two facilities may use AIDC for oceanic ATC coordination?

choices.text: The capability must be implemented, a regional Interface Control Document must govern its use, and a letter of agreement must support it between the facilities.
Only the hardware must be installed; no documentation or agreements are required.
A verbal agreement between the facility managers is sufficient, regardless of regional documents.
AIDC may not be used for ATC coordination under any circumstances per 8-2-3.

items.explanation: Per 8-2-3, AIDC use for ATC coordination requires regional Interface Control Documents and letters of agreement between the facilities.

items.situation: Your facility has AIDC capability with an adjacent oceanic facility, the hardware is operational, but no regional Interface Control Document or LOA governs how the data communications should be used for coordination.
items.para_context: Under 8-2-3, where interfacility data communications capability has been implemented, its use for ATC coordination should be accomplished in accordance with regional Interface Control Documents and supported by letters of agreement between the facilities concerned.

choices.text: Do not use AIDC for coordination until the regional Interface Control Document and supporting LOA are in place.
Begin using AIDC immediately because the hardware is ready and documentation is optional.
Use AIDC in a limited capacity and document procedures after the fact.
Use AIDC only for weather data and never for coordination, regardless of documents.

items.explanation: Per 8-2-3, AIDC use for coordination is governed by regional Interface Control Documents and supported by LOAs. Without these, the proper framework for coordination is not established.
